JOURNAL (previous week):
# Journal — Investing Agent

## 1. Market view

Universe remains broadly soft; cash at 74%, portfolio Core-only across five positions and four sectors. Selective deployment continues — only the Atrium Ljungberg setup cleared the bar this week, on a clean forced-seller/informed-insider setup. Three earnings prints land in a tight July window (Volvo ~24 Jul, Essity ~24 Jul, Thule ~29 Jul); current sizing into that cluster is deliberately conservative. No urgency to deploy further until those prints and/or a compelling new signal.

## 2. Holdings

**HM-B.ST** — Core | Consumer Discretionary | 4.8%
- **Thesis:** Karl-Johan Persson bought ~1.2 BSEK across six transactions in eight days into an 11% drawdown — among the strongest insider signals possible on a Swedish Large Cap. Starter position pending fundamental confirmation.
- **Sell if:** Profit warning, gross margin collapse, or confirmed structural market-share loss to Shein/Temu. Not on price weakness alone.
- **Status:** `intact`

**ESSITY-B.ST** — Core | Consumer Staples | 7.7%
- **Thesis:** Exceptional 90-day insider cluster — 512 MSEK, 7 buyers, 0 sellers — led by Industrivärden and extended by Martinson (129 MSEK, 26 May). Defensive staples compounder at ~14x P/E with 3.4% yield.
- **Sell if:** Sustained margin deterioration where input costs structurally exceed pricing power, or Q2 print (~24 Jul) shows revenue decline accelerating with margin compression. Not on price weakness alone.
- **Status:** `intact`

**THULE.ST** — Core | Consumer Discretionary | 3.6%
- **Thesis:** Ten insiders including chair, vice chair, CEO, and CFO bought ~14–25 MSEK across two waves with zero sellers — rare breadth on a quality mid-cap brand 28% off highs. 3.9% yield and 16% operating margin confirm franchise quality; weakness is cyclical not structural.
- **Sell if:** Insider turns net seller, operating margin compresses below ~13%, or Q2 report (~29 Jul) reveals genuine earnings deterioration rather than cyclical softness.
- **Status:** `intact`

**VOLV-B.ST** — Core | Industrials | 4.8%
- **Thesis:** Industrivärden deployed ~290 MSEK in a single day near 52-week highs — highest-information signal in this universe. Net cash ~58 BSEK, ~4% yield, 9.8% operating margin despite -9.5% YoY revenue. Controlling owner buying near highs is a strong forward-earnings endorsement.
- **Sell if:** Industrivärden becomes net seller, profit warning, or Q2 print (~24 Jul) reveals order intake collapse rather than cyclical normalisation. Revenue decline alone is not a sell trigger if margins hold.
- **Status:** `intact`

**ATRLJ-B.ST** — Core | Real Estate | 5.0%
- **Thesis:** Tagehus (controlling family) bought ~30 MSEK into Varma's forced 7%-discount block exit — classic informed-insider-vs-mechanical-seller asymmetry. Ericsson 58k sqm Hagastaden anchor lease provides fundamental confirmation. Starter size only given high leverage (net debt 101% equity) and unverifiable NAV/cap-rate sensitivity.
- **Sell if:** Further forced-seller overhang materialises in size and insiders do not respond; Q2 print (~10 Jul) reveals deteriorating occupancy or lease renewals; or thesis on Ericsson lease proves less economically material than signalled. Not on price weakness alone.
- **Status:** `intact`

## 3. Watchlist

**NORION.ST** — Selin's 30 MSEK personal buy + 500 MSEK buyback + resolved FSA overhang at P/E 8.5. Pass for now — three July earnings prints already clustered; revisit post–Jul 23 earnings to assess credit quality and NIM trend.

**ASSA-B.ST** — Ten-person management cluster (~83 MSEK, Apr 29) on a quality compounder. Still ~3.6% above 315 SEK entry target; Latour overhang persists. Reassess after Q2 print ~Jul 28.

**VIT-B.ST** — Board member + CFO buying into a 48% drawdown on a quality vertical-SaaS compounder. Blocked by new Capital Fund short and negative FCF (-510 MSEK R12). Wait for Jul 23 earnings to clarify cash conversion and hear management address the short. Track Capital Fund thesis if published.

**LIFCO-B.ST** — Four-buyer cluster (~13 MSEK, post-Q1) on a quality serial acquirer; still above 270 SEK entry target. P/E 36.5 and FCF yield 0.8% demand patience. Watch Q2 ~Jul 24.

**SCST.ST** — Exceptional 8-buyer / 30 MSEK cluster led by strategic peer Grupo Lusiaves, but stock ran +59% over 12m and is +25% above 200MA — entry at current levels would be chasing. Revisit if price pulls back to ~135 SEK or post-Jul 28 earnings.

## 4. Lessons learned / open questions

- **Forced-seller vs fundamental-seller distinction matters:** ATRLJ entry was gated on identifying Varma's exit as technical (block liquidation at discount) rather than fundamental — this is the right analytical step before acting on any drawdown + insider combo. Always ask "why is the other side selling?"
- **Industrivärden as signal amplifier:** When Industrivärden participates (Essity, Volvo), it is categorically higher-information than a single management buy — strategic, long-duration, non-tax-driven holder with full board visibility. Weight distinctly.
- **Screener bug — net insider flow (unresolved):** Screener still summing gross Förvärv while ignoring same-day Avyttring. Confirmed false positives on SOBI, AAK, BILL. Manual cross-check required on every new cluster signal until fixed.
- **Open question:** Volvo, Essity, and Thule all report within a ~7-day window (Jul 23–29). Current sizing (4.8%, 7.7%, 3.6%) is conservative but concentrated in time. Consider whether any light trimming ahead of that window is warranted, or whether conviction holds at current sizes.

## Picks

**1. CORE-A.ST / CORE-B.ST — Corem Property Group**
Rutger Arnhult bought ~216 MSEK across four transactions Jun 10 — the second-largest insider buy in the universe this week after EQT.
